title: Difference Line
description: Stand between 1775 and 2026, hear where their pulse codes disagree, and replay the differences before the border breaks.

howToPlayJson.title: Difference Line
howToPlayJson.objective: Survive a fast 2-minute run by memorizing two short beat patterns, then tapping only the beats that differ on the left and right rails while cashing in brief center "between" windows for bonus combo.

controls.outcome: Places the next beat that belongs only to 1775.
Places the next beat that belongs only to 2026.

controls.input: Tap and hold the center rail when it lights
controls.outcome: Banks a shared beat during twist windows and boosts combo.

steps: A round previews 2 synced pulse strings: a fife-and-snare pattern for 1775 on the left and a sharp synth pattern for 2026 on the right.
Matching beats visually cancel into the divider, so the player memorizes only the beats that are different.
The player replays those different beats in order by tapping left or right as the response bar sweeps forward.
Every few seconds, a glowing Between Window appears: one normally canceled shared beat must now be held on the center rail for bonus points.
Each cleared round adds one more beat, increases playback speed, and shortens the replay timer.
Three mistakes fill the fracture meter and the border snaps, ending the run.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Correct beats score points, faster replays add a speed bonus, and every perfect round raises a Difference Combo multiplier up to x5. Center holds during Between Windows give a larger bonus and preserve combo. The game is
tips: Listen for instrument identity first: old-time drums mean left, synth hits mean right.
Do not memorize the whole pattern; memorize what changed between the two sides.
Use the center rail only when it flashes, because shared beats are traps the rest of the time.
Short perfect streaks matter more than survival because the multiplier ramps quickly.
